frameworkMapping.routePattern: Convert :id or wildcard routes to gofly {id} path parameters and verify path parsing with invalid request smoke.
frameworkMapping.bindingPattern: Move ShouldBind-style path, query, header, and JSON body structs to ctx.BindRequest with portable validate tags.
frameworkMapping.middlewarePattern: Port gin middleware chains one concern at a time, mapping auth, CORS, observability, CSRF, session, SSE, and WebSocket behavior to gofly middleware examples.
frameworkMapping.errorPattern: Translate binding and validation failures to rest.ErrorResponse with code invalid_argument instead of framework-specific text parsing.
frameworkMapping.openAPIPattern: Declare RequestBody, parameters, responses, and rest.DefaultErrorResponses before publishing /openapi.json.
frameworkMapping.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the Gin router serving traffic until sampled status codes, error envelopes, middleware behavior, and OpenAPI schema output match the migration contract.

frameworkMapping.routePattern: Convert :id routes to gofly {id} path parameters and verify route groups preserve public path shape.
frameworkMapping.bindingPattern: Move Echo binder and validator DTOs to ctx.BindRequest, keeping query, header, path, and JSON tag names stable.
frameworkMapping.middlewarePattern: Port Echo middleware in the same order as the gofly HTTP middleware matrix: recovery, request id, tracing, logging, metrics, browser safety, auth, and validation.
frameworkMapping.errorPattern: Map Echo HTTPError validation responses to rest.ErrorResponse so clients rely on code, status, and fields.
frameworkMapping.openAPIPattern: Expose equivalent parameter and body schemas through gofly route metadata before switching SDK generation.
frameworkMapping.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep Echo as the serving router when parameter names, validation fields, or generated OpenAPI schema differ.

frameworkMapping.routePattern: Convert :id routes to gofly {id} path parameters and treat Fiber wildcard or mounted routes as explicit gofly route registrations.
frameworkMapping.bindingPattern: Move Fiber body/query/params parsing into one request DTO bound by ctx.BindRequest, preserving JSON and query names.
frameworkMapping.middlewarePattern: Port Fiber middleware through the gofly middleware matrix and keep body size, CORS credentials, auth, sessions, metrics, SSE, and WebSocket bounds explicit.
frameworkMapping.errorPattern: Map Fiber validation and parser errors to rest.ErrorResponse invalid_argument instead of returning raw parser text.
frameworkMapping.openAPIPattern: Document request bodies, parameter constraints, and default error responses before moving clients to gofly OpenAPI output.
frameworkMapping.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep Fiber traffic active until body parsing, CORS, auth, realtime bounds, and OpenAPI output pass local smoke.

frameworkMapping.routePattern: Convert Hertz parameter routes to gofly {id} path parameters and verify route groups keep the same externally visible path.
frameworkMapping.bindingPattern: Move Hertz binding and validation DTOs to ctx.BindRequest with path, query, header, body, and portable validate tags.
frameworkMapping.middlewarePattern: Port Hertz middleware by preserving recovery, tracing, logging, metrics, timeout, auth, browser safety, streaming, and WebSocket ordering.
frameworkMapping.errorPattern: Map Hertz bind and validate failures to the stable rest.ErrorResponse envelope before changing clients or gateways.
frameworkMapping.openAPIPattern: Use gofly route metadata to expose equivalent request, response, and default error schemas for generated client review.
frameworkMapping.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep Hertz on latency-sensitive HTTP paths until benchmark, binding, middleware, and OpenAPI evidence all justify the switch.

migrationSteps.adopterAction: Map every source framework route to a gofly route path, then verify path parameter names and sampled status codes before changing traffic.
migrationSteps.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the old router active if path names, wildcard behavior, or route group prefixes diverge.

migrationSteps.adopterAction: Move body, path, query, and header parsing into request DTOs covered by ctx.BindRequest and the invalid request smoke matrix.
migrationSteps.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous handler if any binding source produces a different field name, status code, or client retry class.

migrationSteps.adopterAction: Port middleware one concern at a time using the matrix ordering, then run example smoke before switching production traffic.
migrationSteps.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous middleware chain when auth, browser safety, observability, streaming, or WebSocket behavior drifts.

migrationSteps.adopterAction: Require clients to assert rest.ErrorResponse code, status, and fields instead of source-framework text messages.
migrationSteps.rollbackOrEscalation: Do not publish the new route if invalid input does not return rest.ErrorResponse invalid_argument.

migrationSteps.adopterAction: Compare request body, parameter, response, and default error schemas before moving client generation to gofly OpenAPI output.
migrationSteps.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep clients on the previous schema source until OpenAPI golden tests and generated-service smoke pass.

migrationSteps.adopterAction: Switch traffic only after route, binding, middleware, error envelope, OpenAPI, and example smoke gates pass in the target branch.
migrationSteps.rollbackOrEscalation: Route traffic back to the source framework when sampled responses, metrics, trace correlation, or schema output drift.
